ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 (Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon) web-shell post-exploitation: w3wp.exe spawning recon/LOLBin chain
ucs.description: Hunts IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning cmd/powershell/recon binaries — the classic post-exploitation tail after APT41 drops China Chopper-style web shells via exploitation of internet-facing apps (Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228, Zoho ManageEngine CVE-2022-47966, Citrix CVE-2019-19781, USAHerds CVE-2021-44207). This is the cluster's signature initial-access → execution → discovery chain documented in the Mandiant/CrowdStrike 'Double Dragon' reporting.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT41 web-shell spawn — w3wp.exe firing recon/LOLBins
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"               // IIS worker = the web-shell host
| where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)                   // recon / shell / LOLBin children
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                            // skip machine-account noise
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 web-shell spawn — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 (CommandLine requires GPO)
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildExe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildExe in (_suspicious_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType, SubjectLogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 IIS Worker Process Spawning Recon LOLBins (Web Shell Indicator)
id: 6c4b8a1e-1f72-4a4b-b8a4-c41a4d4d4a41
status: experimental
description: Detects w3wp.exe spawning suspicious child processes — APT41 / Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon signature post-exploitation pattern after dropping China Chopper-style web shells via Log4Shell, Zoho ManageEngine CVE-2022-47966, Citrix CVE-2019-19781, or USAHerds CVE-2021-44207.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IIS apps that legitimately shell out (rare — investigate paths and command lines)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41 has been documented across Mandiant, CrowdStrike (Wicked Panda), and Microsoft (Brass Typhoon) reporting as the most prolific user of web-shell deployment after exploiting public-facing apps. w3wp.exe parent + recon/cmd child is the immediate post-shell behaviour their operators consistently use (whoami → systeminfo → net group /domain → certutil/bitsadmin to stage tooling). The combination

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 DLL side-loading via vendor-signed binary in user-writable directory (DUSTTRAP/StealthVector loader)
ucs.description: Hunts a vendor-signed legitimate executable (Google, VMware, McAfee, Trend Micro, Cisco, etc.) running from a user-writable path and loading a co-located, non-Microsoft DLL — the canonical loader pattern for APT41's StealthVector, StealthMutant, DUSTPAN, and DUSTTRAP families. Mandiant's 2024 'DUSTTRAP' research and Trend Micro's 'Earth Baku' tracking both attribute this exact tradecraft to APT41/Brass Typhoon.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as proc_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\Music\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\Pictures\\*") Processes.process_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","vmnat.exe","vmware.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","McUtil.exe","TmDbgLog.exe","WaSetup.exe","cookie_exporter.exe","elevation_service.exe","BDReinit.exe","USBDeviceService.exe","VsGraphicsDesktopEngine.exe","NVIDIA Web Helper.exe","hphmdl08.exe","AppLaunch.exe","PresentationHost.exe","AcroRd32.exe","hkcmd.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT41 DLL side-load — signed vendor EXE running from user-writable dir, loading a same-folder DLL whose hash != EXE hash
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Users\Music\", @"\Users\Pictures\"]);
let _abused_vendors = dynamic(["Google","VMware","McAfee","Cisco","TeamViewer","Trend Micro","NVIDIA","Adobe","Hewlett-Packard","Bitdefender","Mozilla","Microsoft Corporation","Citrix"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_abused_vendors)
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath                       // DLL co-located with EXE
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256                                // not the EXE itself
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\"                    // exclude OS DLL cache
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), Loads = count(),
            Hosts = dcount(DeviceId),
            SampleHosts = make_set(DeviceName, 5)
            by InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
               InitiatingProcessFolderPath, FileName, SHA256
| where Hosts <= 3                                                       // rare = suspicious
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 DLL side-load — Sysmon EID 7 (Image Loaded) via WindowsEvent
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Users\Music\", @"\Users\Pictures\"]);
let _abused_vendors = dynamic(["Google","VMware","McAfee","Cisco","TeamViewer","Trend Micro","NVIDIA","Adobe","Hewlett-Packard","Bitdefender","Mozilla","Microsoft Corporation","Citrix"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7                                                    // ImageLoad
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         ImageHash   = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Company     = tostring(EventData.Company)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where Image has_any (_user_writable)
| where Image !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where Image !startswith @"C:\Windows"
| where Company has_any (_abused_vendors)
| extend ImageDir   = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| extend LoadedDir  = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where tolower(ImageDir) == tolower(LoadedDir)                          // co-located
| where Signed =~ "false" or isempty(Signed)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ImageLoaded, Company, Signed, ImageHash
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), Loads = count(), Hosts = dcount(Computer),
            SampleHosts = make_set(Computer, 5)
            by Image, Company, ImageLoaded
| where Hosts <= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading through legitimate, vendor-signed binaries dropped into user-writable paths is APT41's most-cited persistence/loader pattern across a decade of incidents — Operation CuckooBees, Earth Baku, the 2024 Mandiant DUSTTRAP campaign, and the StealthVector/StealthMutant family. The triple constraint (signed-but-relocated vendor EXE + co-located non-MS DLL + low-host-count) collapses norma

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.
